mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling to reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Approved in FLT3+ AML; listed as PKC/FLT3 inhibitor

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL kinases to suppress FLT3-driven AML growth
additional_notes: Approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3+ AML

mechanism: Selective FLT3 inhibition to block FLT3-ITD signaling
additional_notes: Approved in some regions/late-stage use; strong MOLM-13 relevance

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PDGFR to reduce proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Investigational/clinical

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition including FLT3 to impair AML cell growth
additional_notes: Also expressed as 0.003–3 µM in text; used off-label in AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 to suppress oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; noted to include FLT3 activity

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases; may suppress subsets of AML and work in combinations
additional_notes: Combinations reported in AML subsets

mechanism: Inhibits JAK-STAT signaling to impact cytokine-driven AML growth
additional_notes: Range given as 30 nM–10 µM; converted here to µM (0.03–10 µM equivalent)

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 to promote apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; MOLM-13 often sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter transcription and induce anti-leukemic effects
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma; AML activity noted

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to modulate gene expression and reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Also listed as SAHA (alternative name); approved for CTCL

mechanism: Inhibits DNA methyltransferase leading to DNA hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Range given as 10 nM–10 µM; effect depends on schedule and may require longer exposure

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA/DNA and inhibits DNMT, affecting methylation and RNA processing
additional_notes: Range given as 30 nM–30 µM; often needs longer exposure for maximal effect

mechanism: Inhibits Aurora A kinase to disrupt mitosis and reduce AML cell viability
additional_notes: Range given as 3 nM–3 µM; clinical oncology drug with AML activity reported

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma; AML activity in subsets

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved proteasome inhibitor

mechanism: Inhibits XPO1/CRM1-mediated nuclear export, retaining tumor suppressors in nucleus and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Range given as 3 nM–1 µM; approved in myeloma/DLBCL; AML trials mentioned

mechanism: Induces apoptosis and differentiation (classically in APL) and can exert broader AML cytotoxicity in vitro
additional_notes: Also abbreviated ATO; approved in APL

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I causing energetic stress and downstream growth inhibition (variable in AML)
additional_notes: Text notes millimolar concentrations are common in vitro

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function; often explored in combination therapies
additional_notes: Text lists 'Chloroquine or Hydroxychloroquine' as alternatives; combo potential noted
